basics.name: Gerald Lee
basics.label: Graduate
basics.summary: Graduate of the University of Hawaii at Manoa with a B.S. in Computer Engineering and an M.S. in Computer Science. Holds a Secret security clearance granted by the Department of Defense (2025).

education.institution: University of Hawaii at Manoa, Honolulu, HI
University of Hawaii at Manoa, Honolulu, HI

highlights: Stood up a naval proprietary software on an air-gapped system that ingests cybersecurity data from sensors, enabling users to query data for proactive intelligence and threat hunting.
Used machine learning (Jupyter Notebooks) to benchmark data queries and test red/blue team cybersecurity scenarios, comparing performance against previous software versions.
Developed a web application as a front-end software engineer using Angular JS and Node JS.
Utilized AWS to share work with teammates and collaborate on code.
Assisted with implementing a Google Maps API to enable users to locate their properties using a search function.
Answered technical questions about ITS services and supported software from walk-in customers.
Assisted walk-in support customers to the ITS Labs.
Troubleshoot customer problems with ITS supported software.

projects.name: AI-Augmented Learning & Metacognitive Skill Research
highlights: Conducted empirical research on AI enhancers and defeaters in software engineering education using Workout-of-the-Day (WOD) exercises.
Analyzed student pass-rate data comparing AI-assisted vs non-AI-assisted workflows.
Designed instructional constraints to promote metacognitive skills such as test design, prompt engineering, and error detection.
Co-authored structured analysis sections including results, discussion, and educational implications.

projects.name: Python LMS Website
highlights: Developed a website for a sponsor to teach young students the basics of Python programming.
Designed the website using Bootstrap for the frontend and implemented Firebase for backend storage.
Organized and led monthly meetings with two teammates and the sponsor to present progress, gather feedback, and address additional requests.

projects.name: Volunteer Ally Website
highlights: Created a website application to better connect volunteers with organizations.
Displayed relevant information specific to volunteer work and facilitated communication between volunteers and organizational contacts.
Built and tested the web application using the Meteor framework and used MongoDB for storing user and organizational information.
Collaborated with five teammates using GitHub for version control and code sharing.

publications.name: Understanding npm Developers' Practices, Challenges, and Recommendations for Secure Package Development
publications.summary: Co-authored peer-reviewed research analyzing AI-assisted development workflows; conducted experimental design, data analysis, and quantitative evaluation of developer performance and decision-making.

awards.title: Secret Security Clearance
awards.summary: Secret-level security clearance granted by the Department of Defense.

awards.title: Music Scholarship
awards.summary: Scholarship awarded to incoming freshmen that are proficient at their instrument and meet the need for people who play that specific instrument.
